$comment: Skyway D&B pack — drum & bass / jungle, 174 BPM, G minor. Frenetic chopped breaks, deep reese sub bass, atmospheric pads, MC vocal stabs. 32 8-bar loops + 16 one-shots. Constraints per docs/audio-kits.md.

items.prompt: Drum & bass amen-break loop, 174 BPM, G minor key context, 8 bars. Chopped amen break: rolling kick on beats 1 and 3, snare on beats 2 and 4, ghost notes between, hi-hat 16ths. Strong, accented kick attack on the downbeat of bar 1 (loudest moment of the loop). All 8 bars play the same chopped break with continuous percussion — no fades, no silent bars, no intro, no outro. Loopable. Classic jungle amen feel. Just drums, no instruments, no bass, no melody, no vocals.

Tech-step drum & bass loop, 174 BPM, G minor key context, 8 bars. Punchy two-step kick: kick on beat 1, snare on beat 3, tight 16th hi-hats throughout. Strong, accented kick attack on the downbeat of bar 1 (loudest moment of the loop). All 8 bars play the same locked groove with continuous percussion — no fades, no silent bars, no intro, no outro. Loopable. Modern tech-step feel. Just drums, no instruments, no bass, no melody, no vocals.

A cappella chopped AAAH vocal stab repeated rhythmically in jungle / liquid D&B style, 174 BPM, G minor, 8 bars. The ONLY syllable in the entire 8-bar loop is AAAH — never any words, never any other vowels, never melodic phrases. PURE VOCAL ONLY — absolutely no instruments, no drums, no bass, no synth, no beat, no instrumental backing whatsoever. Each AAAH is a SHORT pitched-up female vocal stab less than 250 ms long, like a producer chopped a single syllable from a soul or reggae record and pitched it up. Pattern: AAAH on every beat (4 stabs per bar), occasionally doubled on the and-of-2, identical pattern repeats across all 8 bars. Stutter / pitched-up texture, NOT a sung pad, NOT a sustained ahhh, NOT a melodic line — short percussive chopped stabs only. Continuous vocal energy across all 8 bars; no fades, no silent bars, no intro, no outro. Loopable.

A cappella jungle / liquid D&B chopped soul vocal stabs, 174 BPM, G minor, 16 bars. Producer-chopped one-syllable vocal fragments in early-2000s liquid funk D&B style. PURE VOCAL ONLY — absolutely no instruments, no drums, no bass, no synth, no beat, no instrumental backing whatsoever. Each chop is a SHORT pitched soul-vocal stab less than 250 ms long, fragments like LOVE, BABY, OOH, YEAH, MMM — never sung lines, never sustained notes, never melodies. Pattern: 4-6 chops per bar at jungle rhythm grid (16th-note swing), the same chop pattern repeats every 2 bars across all 16 bars. Pitched-up smoky soul texture, like a producer chopped a vintage soul record at 174 BPM. NOT a sung hook, NOT a sustained ahhh, NOT a melodic phrase — strictly short percussive chopped stabs. Continuous vocal energy across all 16 bars; no fades, no silent bars, no intro, no outro. Loopable.

A cappella ISOLATED pitched-up female HEY stab repeated rhythmically, 174 BPM, G minor, 32 bars. The ONLY sound in the entire 32-bar loop is the word HEY in a female voice — never any other syllables, never any instrument whatsoever. STRICTLY VOCAL TRACK ONLY — absolutely no kick drum, no snare, no hi-hat, no bass, no sub-bass, no synth, no piano, no pad, no ambience, no reverb tail, no rhythmic backing of any kind. NOTHING below 100 Hz must be present in the output — no thump, no boom, no rumble, no low-end. The female voice should sit alone in mono with no accompaniment whatsoever. Each HEY is a SHORT pitched-up vocal-only stab less than 200 ms long. Pattern: HEY on beats 1 and 3 of every bar across all 32 bars. Imagine an a cappella vocal stem isolated from a track in audio software — only the voice exists in the file. Continuous vocal energy across all 32 bars; no fades, no silent bars, no intro, no outro. Loopable.
